movie.title: Mortal Kombat II
movie.overview: The fan favorite champions—now joined by Johnny Cage himself—are pitted against one another in the ultimate, no-holds barred, gory battle to defeat the dark rule of Shao Kahn that threatens the very existence of the Earthrealm and its defenders.

alternativeTitles.title: มอร์ทัล คอมแบท 2

reviews.author: CinemaSerf
reviews.content: Remember the start of "Galaxy Quest" (1999) when a group of actors are drafted in to help rescue the universe from a nasty megalomaniac bent on intergalactic domination? Well, this time it's poor old "Johnny Cage" (Karl Urban) who's the past his sell-by-date Thespian reluctantly recruited by "Lord Raiden" (Tadanobu Asano) to help save the Earth realm from certain doom. He is to join four other somewhat sceptical fighters, all with various skills, to combat the five soldiers of "Shao Kahn" (Martyn Ford) who has been hitherto indestructable as the princess "Kitana" (Adeline Rudolph) can attest having seen her father slain when her own kingdom fell. Now adopted and trained by this nemesis, and together with her formidable friend "Jade" (Tati Gabrielle), she looks certain to reduce "Johnny" and his cohort to dust. Things become even more doom-laden when an all-powerful amulet is accidentally delivered by Aussie rogue "Kano" (Josh Lawson) into the hands of the khan and getting this sorted out is going to take them all to the perilous netherworld for quite a battle royal. Now this is really quite a good fun action adventure if you take it on face value, and it is certainly no worse than than the first of these Simon McQuoid outings from five years ago, but the dialogue is pretty torrid and even though he looks like his tongue is fixed firmly in his cheek, Urban just hasn't the charisma to pull this off and so he does really rather let this down. That said, along the way there are plenty of homages to other films in this genre; the visual effects are pretty spectactular and there are loads of perfectly choreographed and acrobatically staged combat scenes that are straight out of "Kung Fu Panda" to keep us entertained as the plot heads in a fairly predicable direction. Not great, no - but it passes two hours easily enough.

reviews.author: userloser
reviews.content:
Unbearably, tooth-achingly bad. 

Now, you can only do so much with a plot of a computer game from the early 90s that has been filmed umpteen times already, but this is a new low. 

And a cleaned up, one-dimensional version of Butcher can't save it. 

Just no.

reviews.author: mgaugy
reviews.content:
On first watch of the trailer, I expected that Johnny Cage would be the centerpiece, capitalizing on Karl Urban's name. While the usual formulaic approach and a bit of convoluted and disjointed script, it did pay homage to the previous film (though not the video game). I did appreciate that they gave mostly equal balance to the different characters, though it lacked backstories to everyone but Cage.

We expect the acting to be fun and not deep (except for Cage), which we expected. The special effects were decent, though a few things could have been better. I like the choice of actors, though Urban is a bit too old for the role; instead of many being young and inexperienced, they went with an older, more seasoned cast which gave the characters more weight. Only the Johnny Cage gave notice to the advanced age. Liu Kang's fire special effects were really overdone, but the rest of the movie was not drowning in special effects. Overall, it was a decent film, with a thin plot, okay acting, but a generally fun time. As for the negative, I would say the disjointed script that didn't really refresh any previous plot points was a bit confusing and took away from the experience.

Highlights: I liked the choice of an older cast instead of a bunch of 20-somethings that didn't add gravitas to the characters.
Lowlights: A couple of SFX misses, confusing subplots, and no resolution for Cage's special abilities.

Acting: 5/10
Special FX: 5/10
Writing: 3/10
Directing/Cinematography: 7/10
Editing: 6/10
Overall Entertainment: 6/10

reviews.author: Sejian
reviews.content:
31 years later, Mortal Kombat (1995) is still the best movie in this franchise.

The internet led me to believe that this was a better movie than the 2021 reboot, and it is, in some aspects, but it's still trash. What the !@#$!

Why is the acting so bad?

Why is the choreography so bad?

Why is Shao Khan a !@#$%?! Am I really meant to believe this guy is a conqueror?! How? !@#$in' Cole Young whooped his arse! Liu Kang whooped his arse! Kitana whooped his arse! I assume plot armor is the only thing that saved him from Kitana's dad, since everyone else whoops his arse!

What the !@#$?!

The Punisher: One Last Kill was trash, this is trash, what's next?

What's releasing next?

What has the internet told me isn't trash that secretly is trash? Obsession?! Maybe Obsession is trash! I'll know soon enough!

I liked Kitana, Kano, and Baraka, but even they didn't escape the trash line delivery.

reviews.author: MovieGuys
reviews.content: Utter crap. Sorry, but I see no point in wasting too many words here. Lead actors who clearly have little to no actual martial arts experience, a disjointed mess of a story, plus, at times, excessive gore, that serves no discernible purpose. One to avoid.

reviews.author: mroogway__
reviews.content: I am somewhat of a fan of the game and think that this level of violence isn't what the films need to show. I think if there was more violence this would have been perfection.

reviews.author: Sierbahnn
reviews.content:
Not a flawless victory, but fun
By no means is this a perfect movie, but it is a fun movie, for sure. There have been a lot of bad Mortal Kombat movies, but this is actually not just a decent homage to the games, but an actual movie, with a beginning, middle, and end, along with characters, arcs, and a storyline. The casting is good, with Karl being the standout, obviously. It is very polished, and it is a fun watch.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: Earthrealm's champions, including Johnny Cage, must fight a brutal, no-holds-barred tournament to stop Shao Kahn from destroying their world.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: Mortal Kombat II brings back the fan-favorite champions and adds Johnny Cage to the roster as they face off in a deadly tournament. Shao Kahn's dark rule threatens the very existence of Earthrealm and its defenders. The fighters engage in gory, no-holds-barred battles to protect their home. Victory is the only way to prevent the annihilation of their realm.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: Mortal Kombat II opens with Earthrealm under grave threat from the tyrant Shao Kahn, whose dark rule seeks to conquer and destroy the world and its protectors. The beloved champions from the previous conflict return, now joined by the arrogant but skilled Hollywood actor Johnny Cage. Together, they are pulled into a new Mortal Kombat tournament where the stakes are higher than ever before. The rules are simple yet brutal: fight to the death with no holds barred, as the fate of Earthrealm hangs in the balance. Shao Kahn deploys his deadliest warriors from Outworld to crush the Earthrealm defenders and merge the realms. Johnny Cage must prove his worth beyond his fame, battling formidable foes in spectacular and gory combat. The champions face internal and external struggles as they learn to trust one another against overwhelming odds. Each victory brings them closer to confronting Shao Kahn himself in a final, decisive battle. The film builds to a climactic showdown where the defenders risk everything to end the dark reign. Ultimately, their unity and strength determine whether Earthrealm survives or falls into eternal darkness.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: Mortal Kombat II is an action-fantasy adventure that reunites Earthrealm's greatest fighters alongside Johnny Cage for a high-stakes tournament against the forces of Shao Kahn. As the emperor's domination threatens all of existence, the heroes must battle through vicious, otherworldly combat to defend their home. Filled with intense action, dark fantasy, and dramatic stakes, the story follows the defenders' quest to overcome a seemingly unstoppable evil and secure the survival of their realm.

story_structure.climax: The ultimate showdown occurs where the champions must overcome Shao Kahn's deadliest challengers to protect their realm.
story_structure.conflict: Shao Kahn's dark rule threatens Earthrealm's existence, forcing the heroes into a brutal, no-holds-barred battle against his champions.
story_structure.resolution: The outcome of the battle determines whether Earthrealm's defenders can halt Shao Kahn's conquest and survive.
story_structure.introduction: The Earthrealm champions regroup and are joined by Johnny Cage as they prepare to face a new tournament against Outworld's forces.
story_structure.risingAction: The fighters are pitted against one another in escalating, gory matches that test their skills and unity while the threat of invasion grows.

characters.name: Johnny Cage

characters.name: Liu Kang
characters.description: A dedicated Earthrealm champion fighting to defeat Shao Kahn and protect the realm.

characters.name: Raiden
characters.description: The thunder god who guides and empowers the Earthrealm defenders in their battle.

characters.name: Sonya Blade
characters.description: A skilled human fighter and champion aiding the resistance against Shao Kahn.

characters.name: Kung Lao
characters.description: A loyal monk and warrior supporting the heroes in the defense of Earthrealm.

characters.name: Shang Tsung
characters.description: A sorcerer aligned with the dark forces threatening Earthrealm.

characters.name: Shao Kahn
characters.description: The tyrannical ruler whose dark rule threatens the existence of Earthrealm and its defenders.

characters.name: Jax Briggs
characters.description: A powerful ally of Sonya Blade fighting alongside Earthrealm's champions.

characters.name: Kitana
characters.description: A conflicted princess torn between her heritage and opposing Shao Kahn's rule.

characters.name: Kano
characters.description: A ruthless mercenary opposing the Earthrealm champions.

faq.answer: The fan favorite champions, now joined by Johnny Cage, battle in a no-holds-barred, gory fight to defeat Shao Kahn's dark rule that threatens Earthrealm and its defenders.
faq.question: What is Mortal Kombat II about?

faq.answer: Yes, Mortal Kombat II is part of the Mortal Kombat film franchise based on the popular video game series.
faq.question: Is this movie part of a franchise?

faq.answer: As of current information, no official confirmation exists regarding a post-credit scene in Mortal Kombat II.
faq.question: Is there a post-credit scene?

faq.answer: The dominant genre is action with strong elements of fantasy and martial arts, featuring graphic violence.
faq.question: What is the dominant genre?

faq.answer: The main characters include returning fan favorite champions from the previous film and Johnny Cage, who joins the roster to fight Shao Kahn.
faq.question: Who are the main characters in the movie?

faq.answer: The main antagonist is Shao Kahn, whose dark rule threatens the existence of Earthrealm.
faq.question: Who is the main antagonist?

faq.answer: No, due to its gory, no-holds-barred battle content and violent themes, it is not suitable for children.
faq.question: Is Mortal Kombat II suitable for children?

faq.answer: Yes, Johnny Cage himself joins the champions in Mortal Kombat II.
faq.question: Does Johnny Cage appear in this film?

faq.answer: The central conflict is the ultimate battle between Earthrealm's defenders and Shao Kahn to stop his domination and save their realm.
faq.question: What is the central conflict of the story?

faq.answer: Yes, it is a sequel continuing the story from the earlier Mortal Kombat film installment.
faq.question: Is Mortal Kombat II a sequel to a previous film?
